sources.title: Mixtral of Experts: A Sparse Mixture of Experts Language Model
ST-MoE: Designing Stable and Transferable Sparse Expert Models
Google Research: Scaling Laws for Sparse Mixture of Experts

result.summary: Mixture of Experts models achieve equivalent quality to dense models at 2-4x fewer active parameters per token, with scaling laws showing MoE benefit increases logarithmically with expert count, though routing instability and expert collapse remain key challenges above 64 experts.
key_findings: Mixtral 8x7B demonstrates that 8 experts with top-2 routing achieves GPT-3.5-level quality while activating only 13B of 47B total parameters per forward pass, a 3.6x efficiency gain

DeepSeekMoE's fine-grained expert segmentation (64 experts, top-6 routing) shows 18% better performance per FLOP than coarse-grained approaches, suggesting optimal expert granularity is finer than previously assumed

Expert collapse (where router converges to using only a subset of experts) becomes severe beyond 64 experts without auxiliary load-balancing losses, with Google's ST-MoE showing stable training requires 0.01-0.1 balancing coefficient
